title: pedicularis crenata subsp. crenatiformis
intro: Pedicularis crenata subsp. crenatiformis, also known as the alpine lousewort, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the mint family and is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Pedicularis crenata subsp. crenatiformis include reducing fever, alleviating headaches, and soothing digestive problems. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into tea or tincture, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
